name: preconditions_unsatisfiable
code:
proof fn phi_1_preconditions_unsatisfiable(va_range: VaRange4K, endpoint_index: EndpointIdx)
    requires
        va_range.wf(),
        va_range.len * 3 + 3 < usize::MAX,
        0 <= endpoint_index < MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS,
    ensures
        false,
{
}
reason: If provable, the combined preconditions are contradictory, making the spec vacuously correct and the function uncallable.

name: va_range_len_forced_zero
code:
proof fn phi_2_va_range_len_forced_zero(va_range: VaRange4K)
    requires
        va_range.wf(),
        va_range.len * 3 + 3 < usize::MAX,
    ensures
        va_range.len == 0,
{
}
reason: If provable, the well-formedness invariant forces zero-length ranges, meaning no pages can ever be mapped by this syscall.

name: no_valid_endpoint_index
code:
proof fn phi_3_no_valid_endpoint_index(endpoint_index: EndpointIdx)
    requires
        0 <= endpoint_index < MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS,
    ensures
        false,
{
}
reason: If provable, MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS is zero (or the bound is contradictory), meaning no valid endpoint index exists and the syscall can never be invoked.

name: va_range_len_under_two
code:
proof fn phi_4_va_range_len_under_two(va_range: VaRange4K)
    requires
        va_range.wf(),
        va_range.len * 3 + 3 < usize::MAX,
    ensures
        va_range.len < 2,
{
}
reason: If provable, the spec constrains va_range to at most 1 page, severely limiting the syscall to trivial cases and preventing meaningful address-space sharing.

name: wf_forces_overflow_conflict
code:
proof fn phi_5_wf_forces_overflow_conflict(va_range: VaRange4K)
    requires
        va_range.wf(),
    ensures
        va_range.len * 3 + 3 >= usize::MAX,
{
}
reason: If provable, va_range.wf() alone implies the overflow guard can never be satisfied, making the preconditions of the syscall mutually contradictory.
